title: NTHU and Top Taiwan Venture Capital Launch NT$1 Billion ($34M USD) "NTHU Future Fund" for Deep Tech Startups - Quantum Computing Report
author: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
text:
NTHU and Top Taiwan Venture Capital Launch NT$1 Billion ($34M USD) “NTHU Future Fund” for Deep Tech Startups

National Tsing Hua University (NTHU) and Top Taiwan Venture Capital Co., Ltd. have officially launched the NTHU Future Fund. Announced during the 2026 Taiwan Venture Capital & Private Equity Annual Conference in Hsinchu, the public-private partnership establishes a long-term capital vehicle specifically tailored to guide deep-tech research outcomes from academic laboratories into commercial industries.

[ NTHU Future Fund Matrix ]
Fund Managers ──► Top Taiwan Venture Capital (LP fund administrator & GP lead).
First Phase Target ──► NT$1 Billion (approx. US$34 Million).
Investment Horizon ──► 15-year patient capital deployment timeline.
Strategic Sectors ──► Semiconductors, quantum technology, AI, clean energy, & biotech.
Priority Outlets ──► NTHU spin-offs, faculty/alumni startups, and USP ecosystem tenants.

15-Year Patient Capital Structure for Long-Horizon Technologies

The commericalization of deep-tech intellectual property—such as novel semiconductor designs and physical qubit processors—is frequently hindered by the short investment horizons of traditional venture capital. Because these technologies require intensive, multi-year laboratory-to-fab validation pipelines, typical 7-to-10-year fund lifecycles cannot support their early-stage development.

The NTHU Future Fund directly mitigates this funding bottleneck through a 15-year investment horizon. Managed under a professional Limited Partnership (LP) framework, the long-term pool is structured to cultivate capital-intensive hardware and software startups from seed to growth stages.

The fund focuses on several key technological areas:

Quantum Technology & AI: Funding specialized quantum algorithms, hardware co-design platforms, and localized machine learning accelerators.

Semiconductor Integration: Backing advanced packaging, silicon photonics, and next-generation chip architectures emerging from Taiwan’s foundational microelectronics cluster.

Clean Energy & Biotech: Promoting novel materials, sustainable fuel technologies, and clinical biotechnology solutions.

Fostering the NTHU University Science Park Ecosystem

The collaboration establishes a new operational blueprint for university-backed investment in Taiwan. While Top Taiwan Venture Capital assumes complete responsibility for fundraising, market governance, and final investment decisions, National Tsing Hua University provides strategic trademark licensing, research assets, and its academic talent pipeline.

The investment committee will prioritize NTHU spin-off startups founded by university faculty, students, and alumni, as well as joint research teams and tenants operating within the NTHU University Science Park (USP) innovation ecosystem.

By integrating university-industry laboratory assets with Top Taiwan Venture Capital’s market networks, corporate partnerships, and public support from the National Science and Technology Council (NSTC) and the National Development Council (NDC), the NTHU Future Fund aims to accelerate technology validation, shorten commercialization lifecycles, and assist Taiwanese deep-tech startups in scaling from localized incubators to international markets.

Review the official joint announcement here, and explore the university’s launch reflections here. For ongoing portfolio updates, regional technology transfers, and physical tenant programs, consult the NTHU University Science Park details here.

July 14, 2026
